description: Fix a race condition in browser_execute_script where the polling function returns 'No result captured' before the injected IIFE has executed, causing intermittent undefined results for async code.

userStories.title: Add execution-started sentinel to the IIFE wrapper
userStories.description: Add a '__execStarted_<uuid>' flag to the IIFE wrapper in adapter-files.ts that is set at the very first line of the IIFE, before the try block. This sentinel tells the polling function whether the IIFE has begun executing.
acceptanceCriteria: The IIFE wrapper sets __ot[__startedKey] = true as its first operation after initializing __ot
The startedKey follows the naming pattern __execStarted_<uuid> matching __execResult_<uuid> and __execAsync_<uuid>
The startedKey is cleaned up alongside __resultKey and __asyncKey in the polling function
The startedKey is cleaned up in the finally block cleanup (extension-commands.ts)

userStories.title: Update polling function to check the started sentinel before returning 'No result captured'
userStories.description: Modify the polling function in extension-commands.ts to check for the __execStarted_<uuid> flag. If the flag is not set, return { pending: true } instead of the erroneous 'No result captured' error. Also update the cleanup code to delete the started key.
acceptanceCriteria: When __execStarted key is absent, the polling function returns { pending: true } instead of { error: 'No result captured' }
When __execStarted key is present and isAsync is false and no result exists, the polling function still returns { error: 'No result captured' } (genuine sync failure)
The __execStarted key is cleaned up in the inline cleanup (Reflect.deleteProperty) alongside resultKey and asyncKey
The __execStarted key is cleaned up in the finally block cleanup alongside resultKey and asyncKey
The startedKey is passed to the polling func and the finally cleanup func via the args array
userStories.notes:
Files:
- platform/browser-extension/src/browser-commands/extension-commands.ts (handleBrowserExecuteScript function, lines 376-508)

Context:
The handleBrowserExecuteScript function extracts the execUuid from the filename and derives resultKey and asyncKey:
```ts
const execUuid = execFile.replace(/^__exec-/, '').replace(/\.js$/, '');
const resultKey = `__execResult_${execUuid}`;
const asyncKey = `__execAsync_${execUuid}`;
```

The polling function (lines 418-451) is injected via `chrome.scripting.executeScript({ func })`. Currently it takes 3 args: `(truncLimit, rKey, aKey)`. The fallback at line 449-450 is:
```ts
// Sync code produced no result (should not happen)
return { pending: false, result: { error: 'No result captured' } };
```

Changes:
1. Add `const startedKey = \`__execStarted_${execUuid}\`;` alongside resultKey and asyncKey (around line 393)
2. Add startedKey as a 4th arg to the polling func: `args: [EXEC_RESULT_TRUNCATION_LIMIT, resultKey, asyncKey, startedKey]`
3. Update the func signature to accept 4 params: `(truncLimit: number, rKey: string, aKey: string, sKey: string)`
4. In the polling func body, after the `if (result && ...)` and `if (isAsync)` checks, add:
   ```ts
   const started = ot[sKey] === true;
   if (!started) return { pending: true };
   ```
   This goes BEFORE the existing `return { pending: false, result: { error: 'No result captured' } };` line.
5. In the inline cleanup (line 441-442), add: `Reflect.deleteProperty(ot, sKey);`
6. In the finally block cleanup function (around line 473-490), add startedKey as a 4th arg and delete it alongside the other keys

The three-state machine becomes:
- __execStarted absent -> IIFE hasn't run yet -> { pending: true } (keep polling)
- __execStarted present, __execAsync true, no __execResult -> async Promise pending -> { pending: true }
- __execResult present -> done -> { pending: false, result }
- __execStarted present, no __execAsync, no __execResult -> genuine sync failure -> { error: 'No result captured' }

Constraints:
- Do NOT change the polling interval (EXEC_POLL_INTERVAL_MS = 50ms)
- Do NOT change the async timeout (EXEC_MAX_ASYNC_WAIT_MS = 10000ms)
- Do NOT change the outer script timeout (SCRIPT_TIMEOUT_MS = 25000ms)
- The finally block cleanup must also clean up the startedKey to prevent global pollution

userStories.title: Verify the fix with E2E tests
userStories.description: Run the full E2E test suite to verify the fix eliminates the flaky failure in 'await fetch returns HTTP status' and that no other tests regress.
acceptanceCriteria: All browser_execute_script E2E tests pass including 'await fetch returns HTTP status'
All browser_execute_script E2E tests pass including 'handles async code (Promises)'
All browser_execute_script E2E tests pass including 'multiple sequential await expressions'
The 'sequential executions leave no leftover state' test still passes (verifying cleanup of the new started key)
The 'concurrent executions on different tabs do not collide' test still passes
The full E2E suite passes with 0 failures
